9:I[13642,["/_next/static/chunks/17mgjxqmeuvto.js","/_next/static/chunks/0d3shmwh5_nmn.js","/_next/static/chunks/0ant411rewcrv.js"],"default"]
a:I[97367,["/_next/static/chunks/17mgjxqmeuvto.js","/_next/static/chunks/0d3shmwh5_nmn.js"],"OutletBoundary"]
b:"$Sreact.suspense"
4:T10b6,<p class="text-[#8a8a8a] leading-loose mb-6" key="0">
There is a moment in the Issey Miyake archives—a video from 1994—where a model steps into a tube of pleated fabric. She raises her arms. The garment expands, accordion-like, revealing its full volume. She lowers her arms. It collapses, following her form like water finding its level.</p><p class="text-[#8a8a8a] leading-loose mb-6" key="1">This is the Pleats Please line at its essence: clothing that respects both structure and fluidity.</p><h2 class="text-2xl font-light mt-16 mb-8" key="2">The Technology of Naturalness</h2><p class="text-[#8a8a8a] leading-loose mb-6" key="3">Issey Miyake's innovation was not aesthetic. It was industrial. He developed a process to pleat fabric after cutting and sewing—a reversal of traditional methods that allowed for three-dimensional, architectural forms.</p><p class="text-[#8a8a8a] leading-loose mb-6" key="4">But the result looks organic. The pleats follow no grid. They radiate, spiral, cascade. The garments look like geological formations—layers compressed over time, ready to be eroded by movement.</p><p class="text-[#8a8a8a] leading-loose mb-6" key="5">This is the Daoist ideal: technology that serves nature. Structure that enables flow.</p><h2 class="text-2xl font-light mt-16 mb-8" key="6">The Body in Space</h2><p class="text-[#8a8a8a] leading-loose mb-6" key="7">Miyake's designs do not cling. They create volume around the body—a micro-architecture of personal space. The wearer is not displayed; they are housed.</p><p class="text-[#8a8a8a] leading-loose mb-6" key="8">This approach resonates with contemporary concerns about privacy, about the pressure of visibility, about the exhaustion of self-presentation. The Miyake garment offers a kind of shelter. It absorbs attention rather than demanding it.</p><h2 class="text-2xl font-light mt-16 mb-8" key="9">The Color of Nature</h2><p class="text-[#8a8a8a] leading-loose mb-6" key="10">The Pleats Please palette is drawn from the natural world, but filtered through memory:</p><ul class="list-disc list-inside space-y-2 mb-6 text-[#8a8a8a]" key="11"><li>**The blue of a photograph of the ocean**, not the ocean itself</li><li>**The green of a leaf seen through frosted glass**</li><li>**The orange of sunset reflected on concrete**</li></ul><p class="text-[#8a8a8a] leading-loose mb-6" key="12">These are colors at one remove. They reference nature while acknowledging mediation. They are honest about being artificial representations of natural phenomena.</p><h2 class="text-2xl font-light mt-16 mb-8" key="13">The Legacy</h2><p class="text-[#8a8a8a] leading-loose mb-6" key="14">Issey Miyake passed in 2022, but his studio continues. The current collections maintain his principles: innovation in service of wearability, technology in service of naturalness, design in service of the body.</p><p class="text-[#8a8a8a] leading-loose mb-6" key="15">The Pleats Please line, now decades old, looks contemporary. It looked contemporary in 1994. It will look contemporary in 2034. This is not because it is timeless—an impossible concept—but because it exists outside of time. It addresses needs (movement, comfort, beauty) that do not change with seasons.</p><h2 class="text-2xl font-light mt-16 mb-8" key="16">The Dao of Pleating</h2><p class="text-[#8a8a8a] leading-loose mb-6" key="17">Consider what a pleat does:</p><ul class="list-disc list-inside space-y-2 mb-6 text-[#8a8a8a]" key="18"><li>It creates structure through repetition</li><li>It allows expansion and compression</li><li>It creates rhythm through regularity</li><li>It transforms flat fabric into three-dimensional form</li></ul><p class="text-[#8a8a8a] leading-loose mb-6" key="19">This is 气 (qi)—the energy that flows through structure. The pleat channels movement while maintaining shape. It is both vessel and energy, form and flow.</p><p class="text-[#8a8a8a] leading-loose mb-6" key="20">老子 wrote of the valley spirit that never dies. Miyake's pleats embody this: the empty space that gives form its function, the void that creates presence.</p><hr class="border-t border-[#e5e5e5] my-12" key="21" /><p class="text-[#8a8a8a] leading-loose mb-6" key="22">*Studio visit and archive research, Tokyo, February 2026.*
    </p>0:{"rsc":["$","$1","c",{"children":[["$","main",null,{"className":"min-h-screen","children":[["$","$L2",null,{}],["$","article",null,{"className":"pt-32 pb-20 px-6","children":["$","div",null,{"className":"max-w-3xl mx-auto","children":[["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/","className":"text-sm tracking-widest uppercase text-[#8a8a8a] hover:text-[#1a1a1a] transition-colors mb-12 inline-block","children":"← Back"}],["$","header",null,{"className":"mb-16","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"flex items-center space-x-4 mb-6","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"text-xs tracking-[0.2em] uppercase text-[#c9a961]","children":"essence"}],["$","span",null,{"className":"text-xs text-[#8a8a8a]","children":["2026.04.10"," · ",11," min read"]}]]}],["$","h1",null,{"className":"text-4xl md:text-5xl font-light leading-tight mb-8","children":"Issey Miyake: The Geometry of Flow"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xl text-[#8a8a8a] leading-relaxed max-w-2xl","children":"How one designer merged technology and nature to create garments that move like water and hold shape like stone."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"w-px h-16 bg-gradient-to-b from-[#1a1a1a] to-transparent mx-auto mb-16"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose prose-lg max-w-none","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$4"}}],"$L5"]}]}],"$L6"]}],["$L7"],"$L8"]}],"isPartial":false,"staleTime":300,"varyParams":null,"buildId":"4pMsfNXifuYP0Ex0UfgHH"}
